messages.content:
which factor cannot contribute to the quality of data:

      Timeliness
      Relevancy
      Validity
      Reliability
All of the factors listed can contribute to the quality of data.

How can we call a row of a data set?

      Value
      Attribute
      Record
      Variable
A row of a data set is commonly referred to as a "record."

Which file format is commonly storing structured data by Comma/tab-separated values:

      txt or xml
      json
      xls or xlsx
      csv or tsv
The file format commonly used for storing structured data by Comma/tab-separated values is "csv or tsv" (comma-separated values or tab-separated values).

Which of the following are the correct ways to make data tidy in Data Cleansing?
(1) Making each cell value unique.
(2) Duplicating rows to fill missing values.
(3) Making each value have its own cell.
(4) Making each attribute have its own column.

The correct ways to make data tidy in Data Cleansing are:

(1) Making each cell value unique.
(3) Making each value have its own cell.
(4) Making each attribute have its own column.

Which of the followings is NOT an example of "Correlation does not imply Causation"?

      Soda totally turns teens into killers
      Chocolate eaters are more slim
      Linkage between ice cream consumption and shark attacks
      One variable causes a change in another variable
      Smokers suffer less repetitive motion disorder

The example that is NOT an example of "Correlation does not imply Causation" is:

(4) One variable causes a change in another variable.

The concept of "Correlation does not imply Causation" cautions against assuming a causal relationship between variables solely based on their correlation.

Which of the following is the most related to Berkson’s Paradox?

      A trend appears in several groups of data but disappears when the groups are combined.
      Discovering a reasonable negative association between multiple traits.
      Only considering inputs from volunteer respondents who answered a survey.
      A false observation of a negative association between two traits.

The option most related to Berkson's Paradox is:

(1) A trend appears in several groups of data but disappears when the groups are combined.

Berkson's Paradox refers to the phenomenon where a correlation or relationship between two variables within separate groups disappears when the groups are combined. It occurs when variables that are individually correlated with a third variable are combined, leading to a seemingly contradictory result.
When performing data processing, we partition data into training set for modelling and ______ set for verification. (One word only)
The word you are looking for is "validation" set.

What model is used when classifying Iris Data Set on Orange?

      Decision Tree
      Curve Fitting
      Linear Regression
      SVM
The model commonly used for classifying the Iris Data Set on Orange is the "Decision Tree" model.

Which of the following are the functions for calculating Linear regression in Excel?

(1) SLOPE()

(2) FLOOR()

(3) CEILING()

(4) INTERCEPT()

The functions for calculating Linear regression in Excel are:

(1) SLOPE()
(4) INTERCEPT()

Which of the following about Data Modeling is TRUE?

      Decision tree is a popular clustering method.
      Regression finds a curve that "best fits" a series of data points.
      Clustering requires data to be labeled.
      "K-means" is a popular classification method.

The statement that is TRUE about Data Modeling is:

Regression finds a curve that "best fits" a series of data points.

Decision tree is a popular classification method, not a clustering method.
Clustering does not require data to be labeled.
"K-means" is a popular clustering method, not a classification method.
